name: azure-maps-search-dotnet
description: Azure Maps SDK for .NET providing geocoding, routing, rendering, geolocation, and weather services
qualityReason: Specific SDK with clear use cases
capabilities: Geocode addresses to coordinates and reverse geocode coordinates to addresses
Calculate route directions with traffic data and multiple travel modes
Generate route matrices for origin-destination pairs
Render map tiles and static map images
Perform IP-based geolocation to determine country/region
Retrieve current weather conditions and forecasts
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Documentation-only skill for Azure Maps SDK in .NET. No executable code present. Static analysis found 0 security issues. Requires Azure credentials (subscription key or Entra ID) which is expected behavior for Azure service integration.
valueStatement: Add location-based services to your .NET applications with Azure Maps SDK. Geocode addresses, calculate routes, render map tiles, and access weather data with enterprise-grade reliability.
limitations: Requires Azure Maps subscription and valid credentials
Subject to Azure Maps rate limits and pricing tiers
Some packages are in beta (pre-release) status

useCases.title: E-commerce Delivery Estimator
useCases.description: Calculate delivery times and distances from warehouses to customer addresses using route matrix API

useCases.title: Travel Planning Application
useCases.description: Generate turn-by-turn directions with traffic-aware routing for road trip planning

useCases.title: Weather-Aware Field Service
useCases.description: Combine location data with weather conditions to optimize field technician schedules

promptTemplates.title: Basic Address Geocoding
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me geocode the address '1 Microsoft Way, Redmond, WA 98052' using Azure Maps SDK for .NET. Show me how to set up the MapsSearchClient with a subscription key and retrieve the coordinates.
promptTemplates.scenario: Beginner: First-time geocoding with Azure Maps

promptTemplates.title: Route Directions with Traffic
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to calculate driving directions from Seattle to Redmond with traffic data enabled. Show me how to use MapsRoutingClient with RouteDirectionOptions to get distance, duration, and turn-by-turn instructions.

promptTemplates.title: Batch Geocoding Multiple Addresses
promptTemplates.prompt: I have a list of 50 customer addresses that need to be geocoded. Show me how to use GetGeocodingBatch with GeocodingQuery objects to process them efficiently in a single request.

promptTemplates.title: Isochrone Analysis for Service Area
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me implement a service area checker that shows all locations reachable within 20 minutes from a given point. Use GetRouteRange with RouteRangeOptions to generate the isochrone polygon.

bestPractices: Use Microsoft Entra ID authentication for production environments instead of subscription keys
Implement exponential backoff retry logic to handle rate limiting (HTTP 429)
Cache geocoding results for frequently accessed addresses to reduce API calls
antiPatterns: Storing subscription keys in source code instead of environment variables or Azure Key Vault
Making individual geocoding requests in a loop instead of using batch operations
Ignoring traffic data for route calculations during peak hours

faq.question: How do I authenticate with Azure Maps in production?
faq.answer: Use Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD) with DefaultAzureCredential for production. This provides better security than subscription keys and supports managed identities for Azure-hosted applications.

faq.question: What are the rate limits for Azure Maps APIs?
faq.answer: Rate limits depend on your pricing tier. Basic tier allows 500 transactions per minute. Implement retry logic with exponential backoff to handle HTTP 429 responses gracefully.

faq.question: Can I use Azure Maps offline or cache tiles?
faq.answer: Azure Maps requires an internet connection for API calls. You can cache geocoding results and map tiles client-side, but ensure compliance with Microsoft's caching policies and terms of service.

faq.question: How do I handle geocoding for international addresses?
faq.answer: Azure Maps supports global geocoding. Use the country parameter in GeocodingQuery to narrow results to a specific country. Results include localized address formats based on the Language setting.

faq.question: What is the difference between immediate and async route matrix?
faq.answer: GetImmediateRouteMatrix is synchronous and supports up to 100 route combinations. GetRouteMatrix is asynchronous and supports up to 700 combinations, returning an operation you poll for completion.

faq.question: Are the Azure Maps .NET packages stable for production?
faq.answer: Most packages are in beta (pre-release) status as of v1.0.1. They are functional but APIs may change. Monitor the Azure SDK GitHub repository for stable release announcements.
